description: Ground truth for Fore AI lead scorer validation. Contains 40 leads with expected tiers. Sources: 7 original calibration leads, 11 SDR-reviewed Airbus leads, 13 SDR-reviewed Boulanger leads, and 9 SDR-reviewed Veepee leads (2026-02-24).

leads.name: Guillaume Tronche
leads.title: Head of Software Quality Assurance and Release Manager
key_signals: Direct QA title: Head of Software QA and Release Manager
11yr+ tenure at Credit Agricole CIB (banking/financial services)
QA tools in skills: HP QuickTest Professional, Team Foundation Server, Agile Testing
Agile methodologies experience
EPITECH engineering degree
Banking industry (target vertical)
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Must NOT flag past employer Acial/Vision IT Group as ESN consultant penalty -- only CURRENT employer matters
Past role as Testing Practice Manager at Vision IT Group is historical, not current
leads.validation_notes: Textbook primary buyer. Must always score A. This is the strongest possible lead -- direct QA title, long tenure at a major bank, QA tooling in skills. The AI must identify him as a direct buyer with budget authority over QA decisions.

leads.name: Nicolas Sorre
leads.title: Head of Smart Factory - Airbus Atlantic
key_signals: Innovation scout with significant budget: ~6M EUR/year R&D roadmap
Startup partnership mandate: Sets up partnerships with tech startups
Software/IT DNA despite manufacturing title: cloud data architecture, AI, web/mobile apps
Agile squads and product approach
Past role: Responsable Services Collaboratifs Web et Mobile (direct software management)
Digital transformation leadership
30-person multifunctional team
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Must NOT trigger cybersecurity red flag -- cybersecurity kits appears in description as one of many IoT projects, not in headline or title
Must NOT be dismissed as manufacturing-only -- his software DNA is strong (web, mobile, cloud, AI)
Smart Factory title should not trigger hardware penalty -- he manages software solutions for the factory
leads.validation_notes: Innovation scout pattern. Despite Smart Factory title sounding manufacturing, he has budget (~6M EUR), startup mandate, and strong software DNA (cloud, AI, web/mobile). The AI must look past the title to the description. Must score A.

leads.name: Jeremy Signoret
leads.title: Vice-President - Technical Product Owner/Project Manager
key_signals: VP-level seniority at private bank (Lombard Odier)
Technical Product Owner managing web applications
J2EE/Spring Boot technical stack -- deep software DNA
Legacy system migration experience -- pain point Fore AI addresses
Banking industry (target vertical)
6yr+ tenure showing decision-making authority
About section explicitly mentions web-based applications in banking
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Must match VP Technical Product Owner persona (Layer 3), NOT C-Level (Layer 4)
Vice-President must NOT trigger C-Level/President detection via substring match
Seniority should be VP (18 pts), not C-Level (20 pts)
leads.validation_notes: VP Technical PO pattern. Web app delivery in banking with strong technical stack. The AI must recognize this as a primary buyer who owns web application delivery and would directly benefit from QA automation. Must score A.

leads.name: Laurent Benatar
leads.title: Directeur general Technologies et Operations
key_signals: Executive sponsor: DG Technologies et Operations at Groupe BPCE (major French banking group)
IT management and digital transformation expertise
Previous CTO/CISO role at Orange (major telco)
Elite education: Telecom Paris Masters degree
Oversees technology and operations strategy for entire banking group
Procurement and operations in scope
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Should be recognized as executive sponsor, not direct QA buyer
Outreach should pitch transformation/efficiency, not features
leads.validation_notes: Executive sponsor at major French bank. Not a direct QA buyer but has authority over all technology and operations. The AI should recognize him as someone to pitch transformation to, not features. Tier B because he is too senior to be the direct buyer but highly influential. Must score B.

leads.name: Sophie Planchais
leads.title: Head of Cloud & DevOps @Airbus
key_signals: Head of Cloud & DevOps -- owns CI/CD pipeline where QA bottlenecks live
Management of Cloud and DevOps teams and strategy
Prior role: Head of Digital Platforms and Transversal Architecture
Digital community development
ENSEEIHT engineering degree (Telecoms/Networks)
anti_patterns_to_avoid: About section mentions SAP GRC/authorizations -- this reflects OLD specialties, not current role
Current role (DevOps) must take priority over About section (SAP legacy)
Recency principle: current Head of Cloud & DevOps trumps historical SAP background
leads.validation_notes: DevOps/CI-CD pipeline owner. QA bottlenecks are her problem even though her title does not say QA. The AI must apply recency principle -- current DevOps role matters more than SAP specialties in About section. Must score B.

leads.name: Cornelius Waidelich
leads.title: Transformation Leader Airbus CTO
key_red_flags: All hardware engineering: airframe engineering, wing manufacturing, manufacturing technologies
CTO trap: CTO at Airbus means physical product technology, not software
Zero software context anywhere in profile
20 years in aeronautical/automotive industry (physical products)
Industrial Engineering degree
MEET platform is about co-working for Engineers and Manufacturing Engineers, not software
Both current and past roles are hardware-focused
anti_patterns_to_avoid: CTO in title must NOT give high tech leadership score -- this is hardware CTO
Transformation in title must NOT trigger digital transformation persona
Factory substring must not falsely match cto -- use word boundary matching
leads.validation_notes: Classic CTO trap. Title says Transformation Leader Airbus CTO which sounds tech, but every description is about airframes, wings, and manufacturing. Zero software DNA. The AI must look at descriptions, not just titles. Must score D.

leads.name: Daryouche Khodai
leads.title: Cybersecurity & Digital Fraud chez BNP Paribas
key_red_flags: Cybersecurity role: Analyste Computer Security Incident Response
CSIRT operations and digital fraud detection
Headline explicitly says Cybersecurity & Digital Fraud
Digital Fraud is NOT Digital Transformation -- completely different domain and budget
Security operations, not software quality
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Digital in Digital Fraud must NOT trigger digital transformation persona match
Past ADM toolset Manager role (Jira, Jenkins) should not override current security role
DevOps tools in past role are not enough to rehabilitate a current cybersecurity persona
leads.validation_notes: Clear anti-persona. Cybersecurity and fraud detection is a completely different domain from software QA. Digital Fraud must not be confused with Digital Transformation. Must score D (ideally 0).

leads.name: Arnaud Raffray
leads.title: VP | Head of IM for Corporate Functions & Transverse Products
key_signals: VP / Head of IM reporting to CIO -- senior IT leadership with budget authority
350 people organization across 8 countries
500+ applications in scope -- enterprise-scale pain around release velocity and regression risk
Major platforms: Skywise (big data/analytics), AI and Advanced Analytics, SAP Finance, Workday, airbus.com web platform
24 years experience in transformation programs
Prior VP HR Transformation & Digital (100 people, 12 reports) -- shows pattern of large-scale digital transformation
Influences platform/tooling strategy and budget allocation
UTT engineering degree
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Not a direct QA buyer but a senior IT transformation/platform owner who sponsors deals
Should be scored as Sponsor, not Primary Buyer
leads.sdr_review_summary: SDR says: That scope strongly matches enterprise-scale pain around release velocity, regression risk, and standardization -- areas where software QA agents can create value. Good target for sponsoring or unblocking a deal.
leads.validation_notes: Senior IT transformation leader with massive scope (350 people, 500+ apps). Strongly matches enterprise-scale QA pain even though he is not the direct QA owner. The AI must recognize the scale of his IM scope and its direct relevance to QA. Must score A.

leads.name: Mathieu Rebut
leads.title: Senior Project Director, Customer Services Digital Transformation
key_signals: Digital Engineering background: development and IT hosting of SW solutions
Skywise Solutions (Airbus data analytics platform) in scope
SAFe methodology transformation and team empowerment
Prior role: Head of Overall System Design with V&V (Verification and Validation) support
Requirement Based Engineering and Model Based System Engineering experience
Computer Engineering skills noted
ISAE-SUPAERO engineering degree (elite French aerospace school)
Supply chain digitalisation leadership
anti_patterns_to_avoid: V&V support is adjacent to QA, not direct QA ownership
Current role is supply chain digitalisation, which is somewhat lateral to QA
leads.sdr_review_summary: SDR says: His background includes leading Digital Engineering with development and IT hosting of software solutions (including teams moving to SAFe), suggesting he will care about delivery velocity, reliability, and operational quality. Relevant for discovery conversation.
leads.validation_notes: Enterprise stakeholder with strong digital engineering background and V&V adjacency. Internal sponsor/influencer rather than direct QA budget owner. SAFe transformation signals awareness of delivery quality. Must score B.

leads.name: Clotilde Marchal
leads.title: Vice President Engineering Change Management
key_signals: VP-level in Engineering Change Management -- governance over engineering processes
Background: Software Developer and Artificial Intelligence Expert (1985-1990)
Senior Manager System and Software Engineering (2002-2005)
INCOSE CSEP certification (Systems Engineering Professional)
ENSIMAG degree (elite French CS/applied math school)
Long career in system/software engineering, process, methods and tools
Cross-divisional leadership at AIRBUS GROUP level
Influence over engineering governance, toolchains, and traceability
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Current title is Change Management, not QA -- she may not own QA budget directly
Should be scored as senior influencer/champion, not direct QA buyer
leads.sdr_review_summary: SDR says: She is potentially relevant for Fore AI outreach, but more as a senior influencer/champion than as the day-to-day buyer for software QA agents. Strong system/software engineering background with governance influence.
leads.validation_notes: VP with deep software engineering roots (developer, AI expert, systems engineering) now in engineering governance. ENSIMAG degree confirms CS foundation. Influence over toolchains and traceability but not direct QA owner. Must score B.

leads.name: Robert Louge
leads.title: VP IT Operational Manager
key_signals: VP IT Operational Manager -- owns IT operational products
Scope: Network, IAM, Hosting, ERP, Digital Workplace, ITOps
Mission focused on operational reliability and digital transformation
Prior role: VP Digital Transformation for Airbus IT
Created first Digital Organization for Airbus
Delivered 50M EUR yearly savings through IT organization
Information System and Network PhD from Toulouse III
8yr+ tenure at Airbus in IT leadership
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Sits above QA -- likely to route you to engineering/quality leaders rather than evaluate product himself
IT operations focus, not software delivery/QA ownership directly
leads.sdr_review_summary: SDR says: He is adjacent to your ICP but not an obvious primary buyer. VP IT Operational Manager responsible for IT operational products like Network, IAM, Hosting, ERP, Digital Workplace. Good sponsor/influencer but sits above QA.
leads.validation_notes: Senior IT operations leader who owns ERP, Digital Workplace, and IT reliability. Creates budget/urgency for better automated testing and safer releases. Adjacent to QA but not direct owner. Must score B.

leads.name: Jochen Hoesch
leads.title: Group Head of AI Strategy & Partnerships
key_signals: Group Head of AI Strategy reporting to EVP Group Strategy -- innovation leadership
Defined and secured Board approval for Airbus AI strategy across all divisions
Launched first consolidated AI investment portfolio
Co-founded Group AI Centre of Excellence
Two-digit million EUR annual savings through AI-enabled optimisation
Strategic partnership with leading European AI provider for GenAI deployment
Prior role: Head of Digital & New Business -- reinvigorated Skywise commercial strategy
LBS executive education
MIT CDO certification
25 years aviation leadership experience
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Innovation/AI leadership does not automatically mean QA buyer
Very C-level/strategy positioning -- may delegate tooling decisions
Aerospace procurement and compliance paths can stall top-down pitches
leads.sdr_review_summary: SDR assessment: Adjacent-relevant as innovation scout with AI budget and mandate. Executive sponsor/referral target rather than main sales path. His AI strategy role means he could pilot QA agent technology if framed as AI-enabled quality improvement.
leads.validation_notes: Innovation scout pattern similar to Nicolas Sorre but more strategy-focused and less hands-on software. Has budget authority over AI initiatives and partnerships. Borderline A/B -- scored B because he is more strategy/referral than direct sponsor of QA tooling. Must score B.

leads.name: Nicolas Merle
leads.title: Sovereign Cloud Program Manager
key_signals: Cloud Transformation Director background -- platform modernization
Head of Cloud Services -- managed cloud strategy and modernization
Cloud architect and finance team management
DevOps/security leadership experience
Cloud modernization ties to CI/CD quality gates
key_red_flags: Current role is Sovereign Cloud Program Manager -- narrow scope, only 3 months tenure
No direct QA/test ownership anywhere in profile
Platform and transformation focus, not software quality
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Cloud infrastructure leadership should not be confused with software QA
Short tenure in current role (3 months) suggests transitional period
leads.sdr_review_summary: SDR says: He is adjacent to your ICP. Use him as a routing node inside Airbus: ask who owns test strategy / SDLC tooling / engineering productivity for teams building on the Airbus cloud platform. Not primary buyer.
leads.validation_notes: Cloud transformation leader useful as a routing node/connector inside Airbus. No direct QA ownership but understands platform delivery. Must score C.

leads.name: Fabrice Renaudeau
leads.title: VP, Head of PDM-CM in IT
key_signals: VP-level PLM/PDM leadership in IT -- enterprise application context
13yr+ tenure at Airbus in same leadership role -- strong influence on vendor selection
Former CIO at Renault -- demonstrates IT executive background
IBM Canada PLM consulting experience
International deployment expertise (France, Japan, Asia, Canada, US)
Organization and business process leadership
key_red_flags: PLM/PDM/Configuration Management focus -- not core software QA
More process/organization oriented than software testing
Better as referral path than demo target
anti_patterns_to_avoid: PLM leadership is adjacent to QA but not the same
Former CIO status should not over-inflate score -- current role is PLM-specific
leads.sdr_review_summary: SDR says: Somewhat relevant but not a top software QA agents target unless pitch is explicitly about testing/assuring quality of enterprise IT/PLM platforms. Use him as referral path inside Airbus.
leads.validation_notes: VP PLM leader with strong IT executive background (former CIO Renault). Relevant if QA pitch maps to PLM ecosystem testing, otherwise a referral path. Must score C.

leads.name: Ludivine Brante
leads.title: VP Procurement for Digital
key_signals: VP Procurement for Digital -- manages digital vendor relationships
~70 FTEs across Europe with coordination in India and North America
~2.5 billion EUR annual spend across software, hardware and services
Extended team member of Airbus EVP Digital
Strategic roadmaps and partnerships for technological dependencies
Quality management background (skills include Quality Assurance, Supplier Quality)
TU Berlin engineering degree
key_red_flags: Procurement role -- not the QA champion or technical evaluator
Not day-to-day owner of testing outcomes
Procurement/operations oriented even with quality management background
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Large budget (2.5B EUR) should not over-inflate score -- this is procurement budget, not QA tooling budget
Quality Assurance in skills refers to supplier quality management, not software QA
leads.sdr_review_summary: SDR says: She is relevant as a procurement/economic-buyer pathway. Strong fit on buying power and governance -- leads digital procurement with large team and spend. But likely not the day-to-day owner of testing outcomes.
leads.validation_notes: Procurement pathway for digital tools at Airbus. Influential once a technical team is already interested, but not the QA champion. Use as Who owns this problem plus procurement process contact. Must score C.

leads.name: Armel Djeukou
leads.title: SVP - Digital Products Ecosystem
key_signals: SVP at Airbus focused on Digital Products Ecosystem -- executive digital leadership
Previously led large-scale digital transformation (digital design/manufacturing/services, digital twins)
MIT CDO certification and Harvard M&A training
Board roles at Airbus subsidiaries (Airbus GmbH, Airbus Operations GmbH, Airbus Aerostructures GmbH)
Scope includes digital and cyber ecosystem for aerospace products
20+ years driving operational turnarounds, innovation, digital transformation
key_red_flags: Very C-level/strategy/transformation positioning -- too senior to own tooling decisions
Will not own QA workflows -- delegates to engineering/quality leaders
Aerospace heavy procurement and compliance paths can stall top-down pitches
anti_patterns_to_avoid: SVP plus Digital title should not automatically score high -- this is strategy/transformation, not QA
Board member roles are governance, not operational QA
leads.sdr_review_summary: SDR says: He is adjacent-relevant but probably not the best primary buyer for software QA agents. Treat him as an executive sponsor/referral target rather than main sales path. His positioning is very C-level/strategy/transformation.
leads.validation_notes: Senior digital transformation executive. Too high-level to be a direct buyer but could be a referral target or executive sponsor if the pitch is framed at transformation level. Must score C.

leads.name: Thomas Barre
leads.title: Semantic mapping leader - Continuity CTO project & PLM of The Future
key_signals: PLM of The Future and digital continuity focus -- engineering toolchain modernization
SAFe certification, Scrum Master and Product Owner experience
Enterprise Architecture modelling and data model services
Prior role: Digital Transformation leader for Nose & Center Fuselages
Hackathon organizer (140 participants) -- innovation mindset
INSA Toulouse engineering degree
8yr+ tenure in Airbus engineering/digital roles
key_red_flags: Semantic mapping / master data models -- not software QA domain
PLM/data/architecture focus, not delivery or testing
Long-tenure engineering leadership rather than software delivery/QA ownership
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Digital Transformation in past title should not over-inflate current relevance -- current role is semantic mapping
SAFe/Scrum experience is good signal but not sufficient alone
leads.sdr_review_summary: SDR says: He could be a useful internal champion/connector inside Airbus if QA agents map to engineering/PLM digitalization use cases. Fit is medium-low for direct buying. Position pitch as QA agents to validate PLM/digital continuity workflows and integration changes.
leads.validation_notes: Useful connector inside Airbus for PLM/digital continuity use cases. Not a direct buyer. SAFe/Scrum experience suggests he may sponsor pilots if value is framed as delivery acceleration. Must score C (low end).

leads.name: Moez Kamoun
leads.title: Directeur Technique et Projets
key_red_flags: Manufacturing engineering focus: methodes, outillage, maintenance, moyens generaux
Industrial/technical management career -- not software
Lean/5S methodology -- manufacturing quality, not software quality
Prior roles: Directeur technique in industrial groups, R&D at Valeo (automotive)
Factory director background
ASME STAMP certification (industrial/mechanical standards)
Skills are all manufacturing/industrial: AMDEC, Lean Manufacturing, 5S, Gestion de projet
Location: Tunisia -- manages Airbus manufacturing site
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Directeur Technique title should not be confused with CTO of a software company
Project management skills should not trigger software PM persona -- context is manufacturing
CentraleSupelec degree should not override clear manufacturing career
leads.sdr_review_summary: SDR says: He is probably not a primary target because his LinkedIn profile is centered on manufacturing/industrial engineering leadership rather than software engineering or test organizations. He could be a useful connector but is not the obvious owner of software testing.
leads.validation_notes: Clear manufacturing engineering profile. Zero software context. All skills and experience point to industrial/manufacturing leadership. Must score D.

leads.name: Jean Jacques Gavory
leads.title: VP Head of Procurement & Supply Chain for Space Systems
key_red_flags: Physical procurement for Space Systems: launchers, satellites, physical parts
Supply chain and commodity management for physical goods
Challenges described as competitiveness/EBIT, quality ppm, on-time delivery for physical products
Prior roles: CPO at ASTRIUM for space launcher procurement, Thales production/procurement
Zero software/IT/digital context in any role
anti_patterns_to_avoid: VP seniority should not inflate score -- domain is completely wrong
Quality ppm and on-time delivery are manufacturing metrics, not software QA
leads.sdr_review_summary: SDR says: Not primary target. Senior procurement/supply-chain leader, not software quality/engineering owner. Could be relevant later as procurement gate if Airbus Defence & Space becomes a real opportunity.
leads.validation_notes: Physical supply chain procurement leader. Zero software context. All metrics and challenges relate to physical goods. Must score D.

leads.name: Benoit MAZEREEUW
leads.title: Directeur de l'Offre Accessoires, de l'innovation de l'Offre et des Clients Pro
key_red_flags: Commercial product-category innovation, not tech innovation
'Innovation de l'Offre' = product assortment strategy, not tech startup scouting
No engineering or IT background
Business/commercial role at a retailer
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Generic 'innovation' keyword must not trigger innovation scout layer without strong tech signals (startup, scouting, POC, veille technologique)
Directeur with 'innovation' in commercial context is not equivalent to a tech innovation scout
leads.sdr_review_summary: He's a business guy. Commercial product category director, not relevant.
leads.validation_notes: Classic retail 'innovation' false positive. Title contains 'innovation' but refers to product offer strategy, not tech startup partnerships. Must score D.

leads.name: Karim Menni
leads.title: Directeur Commerce Digital
key_red_flags: 'Commerce Digital' = e-commerce revenue strategy, not software engineering
No tech or IT background
Business/commercial role: owns revenue metrics, not software delivery
anti_patterns_to_avoid: 'Digital' in 'Commerce Digital' must not trigger digital transformation persona
E-commerce context in company descriptions must not inflate software DNA for a commercial director
leads.sdr_review_summary: Business oriented, no tech background.
leads.validation_notes: Retail e-commerce commercial director. 'Commerce Digital' is a business role, not a software engineering one. Must score D.

leads.name: Yohann DJAFER-CHERIF
leads.title: Product Manager | Self&Care | Expérience Digitale
key_red_flags: Product Manager in CX/UX domain: 'Expérience Digitale' = digital customer experience
'Self&Care' = customer self-service journeys, not software delivery
No tech oversight, no software engineering background
anti_patterns_to_avoid: 'Product Manager' must not score high without software delivery or engineering context
'Expérience Digitale' is a CX role, not a digital transformation or software role
leads.sdr_review_summary: Too business oriented, no clear tech oversight.
leads.validation_notes: CX product manager. Title sounds digital but scope is customer experience journeys. No software delivery ownership. Must score D.

leads.name: Cédric Cambier
leads.title: Directeur Expérience et Exploitation Digitale
key_red_flags: 'Expérience et Exploitation Digitale' = digital customer experience operations
Business/CX director, not IT or software engineering director
No software engineering or technical delivery background
anti_patterns_to_avoid: 'Digital' in title must not trigger digital transformation persona for CX/UX directors
'Exploitation Digitale' in retail CX context = digital customer touchpoint operations, not IT ops
leads.sdr_review_summary: No software related. Profile is very business oriented.
leads.validation_notes: CX/digital operations director at a retailer. Despite 'digital' in title, this is a customer experience role with no software delivery ownership. Must score D.

leads.name: Cedric BLONDEL
leads.title: Business Owner Satisfaction Client
key_red_flags: 'Satisfaction Client' = NPS/CX metrics ownership, not software quality
'Business Owner' without software or engineering context = commercial role
No IT or software background despite working at an e-commerce company
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Working at an e-commerce company must not generate software DNA for a purely commercial role
Past Product Owner role in CX context does not qualify as software QA buyer
leads.sdr_review_summary: No software related. Profile is very business oriented.
leads.validation_notes: Customer satisfaction owner at a retailer. Pure CX/business role, no software delivery involvement. Must score D.

leads.name: Quentin Goffinon
leads.title: Deputy (Adjoint) Chief Data Officer @Boulanger
key_signals: Deputy Chief Data Officer at a large French tech-forward retailer
CDO role directly linked to software quality: data pipelines, data engineering, data governance
Technical executive with decision-making authority over data tooling and platforms
Data quality = software quality — strong QA adjacency
anti_patterns_to_avoid: CDO must not fall into 'C-Level unclear tech scope' (15 pts) — should be recognized as executive sponsor (28 pts)
Tier A expected with enriched data (full LinkedIn profile); with unenriched Leads Finder data, B tier is acceptable
leads.sdr_review_summary: Low A tier. Chief Data Officer is tightly linked to software QA. Has decision making therefore a priority in outreach.
leads.validation_notes: Deputy CDO at Boulanger. CDOs own data pipelines and engineering teams — tightly linked to software quality. With full LinkedIn enrichment must score A. Minimum B without enrichment.

leads.name: Ambre BEULENS
leads.title: Business Product Owner Navigation B.com
key_signals: Product Owner for website navigation — direct web app responsibility
Owns the B.com navigation experience = software product delivery
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Should NOT score A — business-oriented PO, not a primary technical buyer
leads.sdr_review_summary: Lower B tier. Business oriented but has clear web app customer journey responsibilities.
leads.validation_notes: Web navigation PO. Has web app delivery scope but is business-oriented. Lower B is the correct range.

leads.name: Hélène Hauguel
leads.title: Product Manager application mobile l Boulanger
key_signals: Product Manager for the Boulanger mobile app — direct software product ownership
Owns mobile app roadmap = knows software QA pain directly
Mobile application context: test coverage and release quality are relevant
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Should not score A — PM of a mobile app, not a QA buyer or tech leader
leads.sdr_review_summary: Higher B tier. Responsible of the mobile app and its roadmap so definitely has knowledge of software QA.
leads.validation_notes: Mobile app product manager. Strong B — owns the product whose quality Fore AI improves, but is not the QA buyer directly.

leads.name: Julien Oet
leads.title: Chief Transformation, Sustainability, AI Officer
key_signals: C-suite AI Officer — executive sponsor with AI mandate
Transformation + AI scope = could pilot AI-powered QA agents
Board member = budget authority
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Not a direct QA buyer — AI/transformation exec, not QA owner
leads.sdr_review_summary: Good sponsor, C-suite AI related. Higher B tier.
leads.validation_notes: C-suite AI and transformation officer. Strong executive sponsor profile for AI-powered QA. Must score B.

leads.name: Yannick Herrmann
leads.title: Finance controlling director
key_signals: Finance Controlling Director with IT/DSI budget oversight (per SDR review)
Oversees IT investment budget = involved in tooling decisions
Digital transformation budget authority
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Title alone ('Finance controlling director') looks non-technical — must rely on enriched profile data for proper scoring
leads.sdr_review_summary: Higher B tier. Related to IT and DSI buying and transformation. Oversees budget.
leads.validation_notes: Finance director with IT budget oversight. Requires enriched LinkedIn data to score properly. With enrichment, B tier is expected.

leads.name: corinne salm
leads.title: Directrice Business Unit
key_red_flags: Business Unit director — unclear tech scope
No specific software or IT context from title alone
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Must not score B or A without enriched data showing IT/tech scope
leads.sdr_review_summary: Meh. C tier.
leads.validation_notes: Generic Business Unit director. Without enriched data showing tech oversight, C tier is appropriate.

leads.name: Antoine Brillon
leads.title: Responsable des Pôles Service Manager Ops et Outils de Production 24/7
key_signals: IT Operations and Production Tools responsibility
'Outils de Production' = production tooling and reliability = QA-adjacent
24/7 service management = release quality and stability are pain points
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Borderline case — enriched data needed to confirm IT context
leads.sdr_review_summary: Lower B tier. Not exactly sure.
leads.validation_notes: IT ops and production tools manager. Lower B range — adjacent to QA via production tooling ownership.

leads.name: Rania KALLEL
leads.title: Lead QA Domain
key_signals: Direct QA Domain Lead title
Actively managing PoC for testing tools (Xray)
Explicit mandate: provide best QA tools and methods
Buying intent clearly stated in description
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Must score 100 — this is the textbook best-case, active PoC manager with buying intent

leads.name: Arnaud BOYENVAL
leads.title: Senior QA Automation Engineer / SDET
key_signals: Direct QA Automation / SDET title
Python, Playwright, Selenium, CI/CD, X-Ray automation stack
Most directly impacted by autonomous QA agents
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Must score ~91 — direct QA practitioner, not 88 or below

leads.name: Minh Quang PHAM
leads.title: Group CTO
key_signals: Group CTO — highest technology decision maker
Explicitly leads AI-first strategy of the group
11+ year tenure at Veepee
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Must NOT score 80 — Group CTO with explicit AI-first mandate scores 90-95, not 80
Do not under-score Group-level C-suite with AI/automation mandate

leads.name: Helmi BOUHLEL
leads.title: Chief Data Officer & Chief of Staff
key_signals: C-level with clear decision authority
Digital transformation mandate at e-commerce enterprise
Business impact focus, budget authority
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Must NOT score 76/B — CDO at enterprise e-commerce is A tier at 83
Do not under-score CDOs; they have budget authority and transformation mandates

leads.name: Flavien Moutawe
leads.title: Engineering Manager | EngineeringOps & AI Automation
key_signals: Uses Playwright and Sonar explicitly
Advocates for quality culture (test pyramid)
Manages 18 developers across 15 product teams
key_red_flags: Engineering Manager, not QA owner — does not own testing decisions
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Must NOT score 99/A — Engineering Manager with QA tool mentions caps at 88
Score ceiling rule: 90+ only for direct QA titles or active PoC managers

leads.name: Marion Mélin-Weiss
leads.title: Head of Group Content – Quality, Responsibility & AI Enablement
key_red_flags: Content quality = quality of catalogs, media, editorial text — NOT software QA
AI enablement here is for content generation, not software testing
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Must NOT score 98/A — 'Quality' in content context is NOT software QA
Content Quality trap: do not confuse content/catalog quality with software engineering quality

leads.name: Christine LU AYEL

key_red_flags: Revenue management, billing, stock accounting — commercial/finance function
Not software engineering or QA

Revenue Management POs are D tier regardless of automation mentions
